---
title: "Data Preparation"
---

<div class="glass-card">
### Data Strategy
[cite_start]Our analysis is based on dynamic data retrieval from the **YÖK Atlas** system[cite: 89]. [cite_start]By using the `thestats` R package (developed by M. Çavuş and O. Aydın), we ensure our study is fully reproducible and updated with the latest 2018-2025 statistics[cite: 90, 91].
</div>

## Data Dictionary

[cite_start]Our dataset consists of time-series information regarding Industrial Engineering programs in Turkey[cite: 91]. Below are the key variables we focus on:

| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| **University_Type** | [cite_start]Categorizes institutions as State or Foundation[cite: 94]. |
| **Base_Score** | [cite_start]The minimum score required for student placement[cite: 91]. |
| **Success_Rank** | [cite_start]The national ranking of the last student placed in the program[cite: 91]. |
| **Academic_Staff** | [cite_start]Total number of professors, associate professors, and doctoral faculty[cite: 91]. |
| **Erasmus_Mobility**| [cite_start]Traffic of students participating in Erasmus exchange programs[cite: 91]. |
| **City** | [cite_start]The geographical location of the university[cite: 84]. |

## Data Acquisition & Preprocessing

[cite_start]The primary goal of this phase is to transform raw data from YÖK Atlas into a tidy format suitable for analysis.

### 1. Data Import
[cite_start]We utilized the `thestats` package to query university data directly into our R environment[cite: 90].

```{r}
#| label: data-import
#| message: false
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show code for data extraction"

library(thestats)
library(tidyverse)

# Example: Extracting 2024 Industrial Engineering data
# raw_data <- list_score(2018:2024) %>% filter(program_name == "Endüstri Mühendisliği")

title: "Data Preparation"

📊 Data Strategy & Sources

Our project focuses on the evolution of Industrial Engineering (IE) departments in Turkey between 2018 and 2025. To ensure scientific accuracy and reproducibility, we retrieve our data directly from official sources using automated tools.

Primary Data Source

The data is sourced from the YÖK Atlas "Lisans Atlası" module, which provides official statistics on university placements in Turkey.

Extraction Tool

We use the thestats R package, developed by M. Çavuş and O. Aydın, to query the YÖK Atlas database. This allows us to maintain a dynamic and updateable workflow.

📖 Data Dictionary

To understand the drivers of departmental success, we focus on the following key variables:

Variable

Description

Type

univ_name

Name of the University

Categorical

u_type

Institution type (State or Foundation)

Categorical

city

The city where the university is located

Categorical

base_score

The minimum score of the last student placed

Numerical

success_rank

National success ranking of the last student

Numerical

staff_count

Total number of academic staff (Prof, Assoc. Prof, etc.)

Numerical

erasmus_traffic

Number of students involved in Erasmus exchange

Numerical

🛠 Data Acquisition & Tidying

The following section contains the technical implementation of our data retrieval process.

#| label: setup-data
#| message: false
#| warning: false
#| code-fold: true
#| code-summary: "Show R Code for Data Import"

# Required libraries
library(thestats)
library(tidyverse)

# Note: In a real scenario, we loop through years 2018 to 2025.
# Here is the structural representation of our data pull:

# data_raw <- list_score(2018:2025) %>%
#   filter(str_detect(program_name, "Endüstri Mühendisliği"))

# Preprocessing steps:
# 1. Clean column names
# 2. Convert scores and ranks to numeric types
# 3. Filter out rows with missing 'base_score' (NAs)


Preprocessing Steps

To prepare the data for analysis, we followed these steps:

Filtering: We isolated "Industrial Engineering" programs, excluding other engineering branches.

Handling NAs: Departments that did not fill their quota or were newly established (lacking base scores) were identified. We removed these from trend analyses to prevent statistical bias.

Standardization: City names and university types were standardized to ensure consistent grouping during the visualization phase.

💾 Final Dataset

We have saved our cleaned and processed dataset for full reproducibility. You can download the .RData file used in our Analysis page below:

📥 Download letithappen_data.RData
(Note: Please ensure this file is placed in your project directory before rendering the Analysis page.)

📝 Ethics & AI Disclosure

AI Usage: This page structure and technical descriptions were drafted with the assistance of AI (Gemini 2.5 Flash).
Prompt Used: "Generate a complete Quarto data.qmd file for an IE department analysis project using thestats package, including a data dictionary table and preprocessing explanations."
Human Intelligence (HI) Statement: All AI-generated code and text were reviewed and verified by Yusuf Bora Çavdar and Onur Furkan Gök to ensure accuracy and alignment with EMU430 course standards.
